attributes.name: Strength
attributes.governs: Raw physical power, weighty pratfalls, how hard a hit lands

attributes.name: Grace
attributes.governs: Agility, physical timing, juggling, controlled falls, dodging

attributes.name: Charisma
attributes.governs: Magnetism, the pull that makes people want to watch you

attributes.name: Poise
attributes.governs: Composure under pressure, holding the Face together

attributes.name: Wit
attributes.governs: Mental speed, comic timing, reading a room fast, quick deception

attributeCreation.note: Player may spend up to 15 points total across the 6 Attributes (1 point = 1 dot), and may spend fewer than 15 if they choose. Applied before age-band modifiers.

skills.name: Brawl
skills.covers: Hand-to-hand combat, a pratfall with real weight

skills.name: Toughness
skills.covers: How much punishment the body absorbs; used as flat soak in combat, never rolled

skills.name: Perception
skills.covers: Mundane awareness: details, body language, ordinary lies

skills.name: Awareness
skills.covers: The craft's sixth sense: sensing rising Suspicion, a failing Bit, a nearby Threshold or power

skills.name: Trade Lore
skills.covers: Knowledge of the hidden world: Troupes, Ring Legends, the Backstage's own history

skillCreation.note: Player may spend up to 18 points across the 9 Skills and Performance, and may spend fewer than 18 if they choose. Performance costs 2 points per dot within this same pool (see performance.costPerDot) — the other nine Skills cost 1 point per dot.

performance.name: Performance
performance.note: Every Clown starts with 1 free dot in Performance (2 if Tent-Born), which can never be bought down. Up to 3 additional dots may be bought at creation, each costing 2 points from the shared 18-point Skill pool. Total at creation is therefore 1-4 dots (2-5 if Tent-Born). It also rises automatically with Rank regardless of points spent; bought dots render as filled circles, Rank-granted dots render as stars in the sheet design.

personalityTraits.name: Messy / Hyperactive
personalityTraits.effect: Takes the face in one minute instead of five, but must roll Perception to remember every prop; on a failure, the look comes out incomplete.

personalityTraits.name: Perfectionist
personalityTraits.effect: Never comes out incomplete by accident, but can never rush getting into character.

personalityTraits.name: Melancholic
personalityTraits.effect: +1 die generating Laughter from genuine sadness or vulnerability; -1 die with pure slapstick nonsense.

personalityTraits.name: Loudmouth
personalityTraits.effect: +1 die to Subterfuge when bluffing on the spot; higher risk of Corpsing if it fails.

personalityTraits.name: Private
personalityTraits.effect: Generates less Suspicion by default; generates Laughter more slowly.

personalityTraits.name: Control Freak
personalityTraits.effect: +1 die on Gifts that impose order or structure; a Flop in a chaotic situation costs an extra point of Face.

personalityTraits.name: Generous
personalityTraits.effect: Recovers Face faster from genuine generosity resolved in play; loses it faster when a Gift is used selfishly.

personalityTraits.name: Reckless Bravery
personalityTraits.effect: One Creative Gambit per session doesn't need table approval; its Face cost on failure doubles.

personalityTraitCreation.custom: May negotiate a custom trait with the MC

specialtyCreation.note: An exceptionally deep Specialty can count as two, granting +2, but counts double against the character's limit.

face.name: Face
face.zeroCondition: Character gets stuck in the mask permanently (the only true death condition in this game).

gettingIntoCharacter.name: Rushed
gettingIntoCharacter.result: Success: complete on time. Success with complication: complete but one detail out of place. Failure: incomplete.

gettingIntoCharacter.name: Lightning
gettingIntoCharacter.result: Same scheme as Rushed, higher risk of incomplete.

tentBorn.rule: Character raised by two Clown parents of different Troupes chooses ONE parent's Troupe as full membership (all passives). From the OTHER parent they inherit a single always-on Heritage Trait, not full membership.

whiteface.name: A Natural Air of Command
whiteface.effect: +1 die when giving someone a direction and expecting it followed

augustes.name: Grew Up Falling Down
augustes.effect: +1 die to Acrobatics for pratfalls and controlled falls

school_of_failure.name: Raised on Flops
school_of_failure.effect: Once per session, downgrade a Corpsing result to a Flop

laughter_doctors.name: A Gentle Hand
laughter_doctors.effect: +1 die when comforting or calming someone in real distress

rebel_clown_army.name: Knows Power When It Sees It
rebel_clown_army.effect: Always know who in a scene actually holds institutional authority

tramp.name: Traveled Light
tramp.effect: One additional Specialty at creation, reflecting a hardscrabble upbringing

new_circus.name: Stage-Trained Young
new_circus.effect: +1 die to Performance in any prepared or staged venue

roll.title: The Roll
roll.text: Whenever a Clown attempts something with real risk, roll a pool of d10s equal to an Attribute plus a Skill, plus one die for each applicable Specialty. Each die showing 8, 9, or 10 is a success. The number of successes tells you whether the action works and how well.

houseDie.title: The House Die
houseDie.text: Alongside your pool, roll one House Die - a single d12 of a different color. It does not add to your pool. It answers a different question: not did it work, but how does the world take it. The MC sets a House Difficulty modifier before the roll based on how easy the audience is to win over. 7-12 (after modifiers) = Cheers, the room is with you. 1-6 = Jeers, the room turns cold.

resultMatrix.title: The Result Matrix
resultMatrix.text: Cross your pool result against your House Die result. Failure is never neutral - it's either the best moment of the scene or the most dangerous one.

rows.cheers: Clean success. Gain 1 Laughter; a Critical Success (3+ successes) also grants the party Momentum.
rows.jeers: It works, but the MC gains 1 Suspicion - a witness, a cost, an uncomfortable complication.

rows.cheers: The Flop. It didn't work - but it was glorious. Narrate the disaster; if the table genuinely laughs, gain 1-2 Laughter anyway.
rows.jeers: Corpsing. It didn't work and it landed badly. The MC gains 2 Suspicion. The only fully bad result on the table.

pushingRolls.title: Two Ways to Push a Roll

creativeGambit.name: The Creative Gambit (before you roll)
creativeGambit.text: Before rolling, describe an unusually bold or clever approach. If the table agrees it genuinely earns it, add +2 dice to your pool. The cost: if the result is any kind of Failure, you lose 1 additional Face, on top of anything else.

encore.name: The Encore (after you roll)
encore.text: After seeing your results, spend 1 Laughter per die to reroll up to 3 dice from your pool that didn't score a success. New results replace the old ones on those dice only. The House Die is never rerolled this way.

turnAndBit.title: Turn and Bit (Double Vision)
turnAndBit.text: Any supernatural Gift used in front of mortals needs both halves of Double Vision. The Turn is what actually happened - the real mechanical effect, resolved with your dice pool as normal. The Bit is the story you tell to cover it. Selling the Bit well (Cheers) keeps things clean; selling it badly (Jeers, especially Corpsing) raises Suspicion. In the Backstage itself, with no mortals present, there's no Bit to sell - Turns simply happen as they are.

resourceGlossary.name: Laughter (0-10)
resourceGlossary.text: Your fuel. Every active Gift costs Laughter to use. Earned by making someone laugh for real, landing a good Flop, and performing for an audience. Does not refill on its own.

resourceGlossary.name: Face (1-10)
resourceGlossary.text: Your soul. Erodes when you use your Gifts cruelly or selfishly, and when you fail badly with no one there to catch it. Restored through genuine generosity, resolved emotional arcs, and (for the Physician) real care given to someone in need. At 0 Face, a character doesn't die - they get stuck in the mask permanently, the only true death condition in this game.

resourceGlossary.name: Renown (1-10)
resourceGlossary.text: Your legend. Character level and a measure of how deeply your name is carved into the Backstage's memory. Never spent, only rises. Determines your Rank and how much of a target you've become - a high-Renown Clown who falls leaves a far more valuable, and more dangerous, Motley behind.

resourceGlossary.name: Suspicion
resourceGlossary.text: The MC's pressure gauge. A shared, table-visible counter the MC gains from Jeers and Corpsing, and spends to activate boss abilities, introduce complications, or summon the Ushers.

resourceGlossary.name: Momentum
resourceGlossary.text: The party's shared spark. Gained from Critical Successes with Cheers, and from certain Gifts (especially the Host's). Spent collectively - to boost another player's roll, trigger a joint Turn, or power a coordinated finishing move.

playStructure.name: The Spotlight
playStructure.text: No numeric initiative. Whoever has the natural reaction to what just happened, acts next. A player's Jeer-Failure (Corpsing) passes the Spotlight to the MC, who may spend Suspicion to let an enemy act.

playStructure.name: Main Action & Minor Actions
playStructure.text: While holding the Spotlight, take one Main Action (a Turn, an attack, a significant move, a complex skill use) plus as many reasonable Minor Actions as fit the moment (a line, a gesture, maintaining a Bit, switching a Stance).

playStructure.name: Scene, Act, Show, Intermission
playStructure.text: A Scene is a unit of focused action. An Act is several Scenes forming its own arc. A Show is a full session. Intermission is everything between sessions - Face recovers slowly, Renown gains are formalized, Debts are paid down.
